title: Law of the Child (Walfare Committee) Regulations, 2015
title_en: Law of the Child (Walfare Committee) Regulations, 2015

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Law of the Child (Welfare Committee ) Regulations.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations, unless the context requires otherwise— “ Act ” means the Law of the Child Act; “ Commissioner ” means the commissioner appointed under section 3 of the Act ; “ Committee ” means the welfare committee established under section 134(2) of the Act ; “ Institution ” means a retention home; “ Minister ” means the Minister responsible for social welfare; “ social welfare officer ” means a social welfare officer in the service of the Government and it includes a social welfare assistant; “ social welfare department ” means the department, section, unit or other administrative body in the local government authority that is responsible for the provision of social welfare services and acts on behalf of the local government authority to safeguard and promote the welfare of children; “ staff member ” means an employee of a retention home or any other person employed in a capacity that involves supervising children in retention home. [Cap. 13] Part II – Establishment

provisions.title: Establishment of a committee
provisions.content: A retention home shall establish a Child Welfare Committee in accordance with section 134(2) of the Act .

provisions.title: Appointment, composition and qualifycation of members of Child Welfare Committee
provisions.content: (1) Members of the Committee shall be appointed by a vacancy advertised in the district by the Head of the Social Welfare Department. (2) The head of the Social Welfare Department shall, before appointing members of the committee, satisfy himself that, the names of person he is appointing has been recommended by the ward development committee after the approval of the Local Government Authority constituted by a panel consisting of the Head of Social Welfare Department in the district, the Regional Social Welfare Officer, the Ward Executive Officer of the respective Ward and the Manager of the respective Retention Home. (3) The Head of Social Welfare Department in the district shall recommend the persons to be appointed members of the Committee to the Commissioner . (4) The Commissioner may, upon receiving the recommendation from the Head of Social Welfare Department in the district, appoint members to the Committee .

provisions.title: Qualification
provisions.content: (1) A person shall not be appointed as a member of the Committee unless that person have knowledge or experience of children’s human rights, child protection and good governance. (2) Any member of a committee shall be a resident of the respective ward in which the retention home is located.

provisions.title: Composition
provisions.content: The Committee shall— (a) consist of not less than four members and not more than ten members; (b) include a member of the Ward Development Committee , Most Vulnerable Children Committee and at least two members from the community preferably two representatives from dominant Faith Based Organisation and any person interested with children issues and at least two members shall be female.

provisions.title: Chairperson and Secretary of the Committee
provisions.content: Members of the Committee shall elect their own Chairperson. (a) the chairperson shall chair all the meetings of the Welfare Committee and may call for an extraordinary meeting of the Welfare Committee ; (b) the Chairperson of the Committee may have the power to request that other persons attend the Committee but such persons shall not have power to vote; and (c) the Manager of the Retention Home shall be the Secretary of the Committee and shall— (i) be responsible for carrying out the decisions of the Committee , the day to day administration and management of the affairs of the Committee in collaboration with the Chairperson; (ii) be responsible for preparing the agenda and for recording and keeping minutes of the proceedings of the Committee ; (iii) perform such other functions as may be necessary to ensure the functioning of the Committee ; (d) the Manager of the Retention Home shall not sit as a Secretary in the deliberation of the proceedings if there is a complaint against him before the Welfare Committee , and another Welfare Committee member shall be elected to act as a Secretary.

provisions.title: Tenure of the Committee
provisions.content: The Committee shall serve for a period of three years. The Chairperson and Members of the Committee shall be eligible for appointment for a maximum of two consecutive terms. Members may resign before their term expires by giving one months notice in writing. The vacancies should be filled using the same appointment procedure as set out in regulation 4 (c) - (e).

provisions.title: Disqualification and abstention of members
provisions.content: (1) A person who any interest in any contract for the supply of goods or services to Retention home shall have not be a member of the Committee . (2) If any member of the Committee is related by consanguinity or affinity, up to the fourth degree inclusive, to any child or has any professional interest in any child, other than as a member of the Committee , he shall forthwith make a declaration of such relationship or interest to the Committee and such declaration shall be entered in the minutes. The said member shall thereafter abstain from taking part in the proceedings and deliberations of the Committee on any question relating to such child and from voting thereon. (3) If any member of the Committee is related by consanguinity or affinity, up to the fourth degree inclusive, to any member of staff of the Retention home, he shall forthwith make a declaration of such relationship or interest to the Committee and such declaration shall be entered in the minutes. (4) The Head of the Social Welfare Department shall consider whether a member of the Committee who falls under sub-regulation (c) has a conflict of interest and is able to undertake his functions with independence and impartiality and in the event that he is not, he shall be requested to resign his membership. (5) A member who absents himself without valid reason for more than four consecutive meetings shall be considered as having resigned his post. (6) A member moves or is transferred from the area where the Retention home is located. (7) A member is unable to discharge his duties for reasons of death, permanent illness or other incapacity. (8) A member is convicted of a criminal offence. (9) A member is in violation of the Code of Conduct of the First Schedule to these Regulations. Part III – Functions and duties

provisions.title: The functions of the Committee
provisions.content: The Committee shall perform the following functions (a) to ensure that the treatment of children, the condition of the premises and the administration of the Retention home meets minimum quality standards set by the Commissioner ; (b) to provide to the Head of the Social Welfare Department of the District in which the Retention home is located with a report every 3 months on the matters contained in sub-regulation (a) (c) to investigate complaints made by any child or member of staff of the Retention Home; (d) to advise the Head of the Social Welfare Department of the District in which the Retention home is located of any matter of concern relating to the care of a child in the Retention home and any issue of concern relating to the organization and improvement of the Retention home or any other matter on which the Committee deems it opportune to tender its advice; (e) to advise the Head of the Social Welfare Department of the District in which the Retention home is located on matters relating to education, vocational activity or the work to be undertaken by any resident child; (f) to inquire into and report upon any matter which deems proper, or the Commissioner requests it to enquire into; and (g) document any action taken in relation to any case of a child or staff member considered by the Committee .

provisions.title: Non-disclosure of information
provisions.content: Committee members shall not disclose information about any child or staff member , save that such information may be disclosed — (a) to the Head of the Social Welfare Department of the District in which the Retention home is located at the discretion of the Committee or on the request of the Head of the Social Welfare Department; (b) to the Commissioner where the Committee deem it appropriate to do so; (c) to a Court, where it so orders; (d) to the police where a crime has been committed against a child; (e) to the Attorney General’s Office and Director of Public Prosecutions during their regular inspections; (f) to Commission for Human Rights and Good Governance regular and periodic inspections; (g) to the Judiciary regular inspections, and (h) to other MDA with the authority to visit and inspect the Retention Homes.

provisions.title: Meetings and proceedings of the Committee
provisions.content: (1) The Committee shall meet at least four times a year at such place as the Committee may from time to time determine. (2) Except where otherwise provided in these regulations, the quorum for meetings shall be two thirds of members of the Committee . (3) The meetings of the Committee shall be presided over by the Chairperson or, in his absence, a member appointed by the Committee to preside over the meeting. (4) Minutes of the proceedings and deliberations of the Committee shall be recorded by the Secretary of the Committee and the minutes or other records shall be kept in the secretary custody. (5) Such minutes shall be read and confirmed at the first meeting after the meeting to which they relate, or any subsequent meeting, and shall be signed by the Chairperson or other member presiding the meeting at which they are confirmed and by the Secretary.

provisions.title: Visits and inspections by the Committee
provisions.content: (1) The Committee shall visit and inspect Retention Homes on quarterly basis and in such manner as the Committee deems convenient. (2) Children shall be informed of any intended visit, of the role of the Committee and of their right to see and speak to a member of the Committee , except when an unannounced visit is made (3) If the Committee so requests, such visits and inspections shall be attended by the Manager and any other officer designated by the Manager. (4) The Committee shall consult with the children accommodated in the Retention Home when it is desirable to do so. (5) The children shall be informed that they may, at any time raise any concern or complaints about their treatment in the Retention Home with any member of the Committee . (6) Any child wishing to raise a concern or make a complaint shall be heard in such part of the Retention Home as the Committee may deem fit. (7) No sanction shall be ordered, applied, permitted or tolerated against any child or staff of the Retention Home for having communicated to the committee any information, whether true or false. (8) Neither the Manager nor any other member of the staff shall be present while a child or staff is making a complaint before the Committee . The Manager, however, shall be informed at the time the Committee deems appropriate of any such complaint. (9) The Committee shall keep a record of all complaints made to it by a child and its decision thereon. (10) a complaint made by a child or by another person on the child's behalf, whether during a visit or inspection or otherwise shall be investigated promptly and no later than 7 days after it is made, and the child shall receive a reply either orally or in writing, as the Committee deems appropriate no later than 14 days after the complaint was made. (11) Where it is not considered practicable by the Committee to carry out a visit or an inspection under the foregoing provisions of this regulation by all the members of the Committee , a visit or an inspection may be carried out by not less than two members, one of whom shall be a non-government member, designated for such purpose by the Committee and this shall be deemed to be a visit or an inspection carried out by the Committee , and the foregoing provisions shall apply to any such visit or inspection in the same manner and to the like effect as to one carried out by the Committee . (12) Any record kept by the members carrying out such a visit or inspection and any complaint received by such members shall be referred to the Committee at its next meeting and entered in the minutes. (13) The members of the Committee may also make such visits or inspections either together or separately at any time, whether by day or night, whenever they consider it desirable to do so. and (14) Such visits or inspections may also be made without prior notice to the Manager. (15) A report of such visits or inspections shall be made to the Committee at its next meeting unless the members carrying out the visit or inspection considers it appropriate to inform the Committee earlier through the Chairperson.

provisions.title: Decisions of the Committee
provisions.content: (1) The decisions of the Committee shall be taken by a majority of the members present and voting. (2) The decisions of the Committee shall not be binding upon the manager but it shall be the duty of the Manager to take serious cognizance of the recommendations of the Committee following a decision taken as provided in sub-regulation (1) and to enter into a dialogue with the Committee on possible implementation measures. (3) When the Manager, or any other member of the staff acting on his behalf, is of the opinion that the recommendations of the Committee cannot be implemented for reasons which are in the best interests of the Retention Home administration, an explanation in writing of these reasons shall, within one month of the date of receipt of the Committee 's recommendations, be forwarded to the Chairperson of the Committee and copied to the Commissioner . (4) The Commissioner may confirm or vary the decision of the Manager.

provisions.title: Official record of visits by Committee or its members
provisions.content: (1) The members of the Committee shall make a note in the official Visitors' Book of every visit or inspection made by them, with such remarks as they deem proper in regard to the Retention Home and the children. (2) The official Visitors' Book shall be produced to the Committee at each quarterly meeting and at such other times as the Committee may require. (3) The Commissioner may request to examine the official Visitors' Book and the minutes of the Committee .

provisions.title: Child protection against abuses in retention home
provisions.content: (1) The Committee shall inform the Commissioner immediately of any child protection concern or any allegation of abuse which comes to its knowledge and shall have power to recommend to the appropriate authority. (a) to take disciplinary action against any staff member , including suspension. (b) to course for the immediate removal of a child from the Retention home where this is necessary for the protection of the child by making an application in the court that committed child to the Retention home.

provisions.title: Inspection of food
provisions.content: The Committee shall arrange for the food of the children to be inspected by a member of the Committee at frequent intervals.

provisions.title: Reports on children health
provisions.content: The Committee shall inquire into any report made to it, or any information otherwise coming to its knowledge, that a child's health, mental or physical, has been or is likely to be injuriously affected by any conditions of his detention.

provisions.title: Access to premises and records
provisions.content: (1) The Committee and every member thereof shall have access at any time to every part of the Retention Home and to every child and may interview any child out of the sight and hearing of any staff members. (2) The Committee and every member thereof shall have access to all Retention Home records.

provisions.title: Compliance
provisions.content: Committee shall ensure that there is compliance with Parts VI, VIII and XI of the Law of the Child (Retention Homes) Rules G.N. No. 151 of 2012.

provisions.title: Annual report
provisions.content: (1) The Committee shall prepare an annual report to the Commissioner at the end of each year concerning the state of the Retention Home and their administration and, generally, the carrying out of its functions. (2) Confidential information collected by the Committee shall be privileged and no personal file shall be published without the express consent of the child or person concerned.

provisions.title: Code of conduct
provisions.content: All staff members of the Committee shall sign and comply with the Code of Conduct on child protection provided in the Schedule adjusted and notified by the Minister in the official Gazette . Schedule Code of Conduct on Child protection I, ________________________________________ member of the Children Welfare Committee, agree that while I am discharging my functions as member of the Welfare Committee in the Retention Home, I will treat children in the Retention Home with respect regardless of race, colour, sex, language, religion, political or other opinion, national, ethnic or social origin, property, disability, health status, birth or other status. 1. I will not use language or behaviour towards children that is inappropriate, harassing, abusive, sexually provocative, labeling, demeaning or culturally inappropriate. 2. I will not engage a child in any form of sexual activity or acts, including paying for sexual services or acts, where under the Penal Code Chapter 16 of the laws as amended. 3. Wherever possible, will ensure that another adult is present when working in the proximity of children. 4. I will not use any computers, mobile phones, video, digital cameras or any other electronic devices while performing my duties in Retention Home. 5. Comply with all relevant laws of the united republic of tanzania. I understand that the onus is on me, as a member of Children Welfare Committee to use commonsense and avoid actions or behaviors that could be construed as child abuse when performing my duties. definitions.definition: the Law of the Child Act
the commissioner appointed under section 3 of the Act
the welfare committee established under section 134(2) of the Act
the Minister responsible for social welfare
a social welfare officer in the service of the Government and it includes a social welfare assistant

the department, section, unit or other administrative body in the local government authority that is responsible for the provision of social welfare services and acts on behalf of the local government authority to safeguard and promote the welfare of children
an employee of a retention home or any other person employed in a capacity that involves supervising children in retention home. [Cap. 13] Part II – Establishment
